selectedLeads.officialSite: none found in OSM public listing reviewed 2026-07-06
publicSignals: OSM way/1301172740 lists 1640 North Cotner Boulevard, Lincoln, NE 68505
Vehicle repair and oil-change service tags; credit/debit accepted tags
selectedLeads.diagnosis: A directory/listing searcher can see the phone and hours, but there is no controlled first-click page to explain repair/oil-change intake, map the Cotner Boulevard location, or make tap-to-call obvious on mobile.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: One-page repair counter landing page with tap-to-call, hours/location, and simple issue routing.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The leak is easy to explain, the public phone route is usable, and the first useful asset is small enough to polish quickly with real photos/services if they respond.

qualityGate.designDifferentiation: Bright garage-counter / clipboard-and-bay-sign motif in cream, teal, and safety orange; uses verified hours/address/phone and avoids the repeated dark contractor hero/right quote-form pattern.
realAssetsFound: OSM service tags for car repair/oil change
unverifiedClaimsAvoided: full service menu beyond public tags
qualityGate.visualQaNotes: Local browser/vision QA completed at desktop width. Initial pass was polished but had a few agency/audit labels, so copy was softened from jargon like money-leak/minimum-fix language to plainer shop-owner wording. Final page remains distinctive, has visible sample-site guardrail, no fake proof, readable sections, and a mobile sticky call path. Production verification pending Vercel deployment.

draft.body: Hi — this is Stark with SYBEORG. I was looking at Lincoln auto repair listings and noticed Ari Auto Repair has a public phone, hours, and Cotner Boulevard address listed, but I did not see an owned website field attached in the listing I checked. I made a no-obligation sample first page that puts the call button, hours, location, and repair/oil-change intake path upfront for mobile visitors. Worth polishing this up with your real photos and wording? If not, reply or tell me NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.diagnosis: A phone-only body-shop listing can lose collision/estimate shoppers to competitors with clearer estimate request pages and photo-upload paths.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Small collision-estimate page: tap-to-call, photo checklist, insurance/estimate flow after verification.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Auto body customers often need fast next steps; the current public signal leaves SYBEORG room to propose a concrete call/photo-estimate funnel.

qualityGate.designDifferentiation: Would need a collision-estimate/photo-upload concept, but public assets were too thin today to make it excellent rather than generic.

draft.body: Hi — this is Stark with SYBEORG. I was checking Omaha auto body listings and noticed Crabtree Auto Body shows a public phone number, but I did not see an owned website field in the listing I checked. For collision/estimate shoppers, a simple mobile page with tap-to-call and a photo/estimate checklist can make the next step clearer. I have notes for a no-obligation sample concept if you want me to polish it up. Worth a quick look? If not, tell me NO and I will not follow up.

publicSignals: Website returned HTTP 200 and title 'Pearson Plumbing, HVAC & Pest Control in Rockford IL'
Listing email christie@rstenstrom.com differs from public website domain pearsonguy.com
Site has tel links and form/estimate language; no mailto detected in HTML probe
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The helpful-alert angle is not that the site is broken; it is that a public listing email appears to use a different domain than the customer-facing site, which can look inconsistent and can fragment tracking/lead ownership.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Contact-route audit: reconcile public email/domain, add clear tracked conversion routes, and verify form/email attribution.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: It is a low-accusation operations fix for an otherwise established service company: helpful, specific, and easy to validate internally.

qualityGate.designDifferentiation: Mockup not created because an alert/audit note is more useful than a speculative redesign for a working site.
unverifiedClaimsAvoided: ownership assumptions about the email domain
qualityGate.visualQaNotes: No visual mockup; recommend a concise screenshot-backed alert before outreach if Stark wants this sent.

draft.subject: small contact-route note for Pearson
draft.body: Hi Pearson team — I was checking Rockford plumbing/HVAC listings and noticed one small contact-route detail that may be worth verifying: the public listing I reviewed surfaced an email at rstenstrom.com while the customer-facing website is pearsonguy.com. Your website itself loaded correctly and has phone/form paths, so this is not a redesign pitch — just a quick consistency/tracking item that could confuse attribution or look unfamiliar to some customers. Worth having me send the exact note/screenshot? Reply NO and I will not follow up.
